Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Beta test ISKCON Media Vedic Library

Tech · asked by user [] · 2008-09-07 · 12 answers
We are currently working on a Vedic Library with books from many vaishnava acaryas, study guides and many other things to read.

Have a look at it here:

http://library.iskconmedia.com

This has not yet been announced so Pariprashnena users will be the first to know. :)

It is a work in progress so keep that in mind.

your servants,
iskcontech team
user [38] · 2008-09-07
Well, we already have most of that on VEDA.
user [33] · 2008-09-07
Excellent! Thanks prabhu!

[quote][cite] admin:[/cite]We are currently working on a Vedic Library with books from many vaishnava acaryas, study guides and many other things to read.

Have a look at it here:

http://library.iskconmedia.com

This has not yet been announced so Pariprashnena users will be the first to know. :)

It is a work in progress so keep that in mind.

your servants,
iskcontech team[/quote]
user [1] · 2008-09-07
[quote][cite] VEDA:[/cite]Well, we already have most of that on VEDA.[/quote]
Actually that is incorrect. 90% of the content in the library are the acaryas books.
http://library.iskconmedia.com/acaryas

There are around 240 books in pdf format which i think is not available anywhere else. Other sections are from veda but here they are presented in a different way.
user [38] · 2008-09-08
I see. I have these too (and more) but didnt make them public because of an unsure situation with copyrights.
user [23] · 2008-09-08
I tried a to download one document last night and this morning, but was unable. I have a dialup connection that is pretty slow. I use a download manager thats worked well for me elsewhere. The document was about 2.5 megabytes. It started at a relatively fast speed, then slowed down a lot and finally stopped without finishing the download. I tried four or five times. Unfortunately the download cannot be resumed, requiring it to be started from the beginning if stopped. Its been a long time since Id previously seen an unresumable download.

Ill try using something else to download when I get the chance.
user [33] · 2008-09-08
So far Ive had no problem. I am on high speed wireless tho.
user [149] · 2008-09-17
Looks good. Thanks. Ive had no problems downloading via broadband.

Will any of Srila Vyasadevs work eventually be listed under Vaisnava Acaryas? Ramanujacarya? Sridhar Swami?
user [154] · 2008-10-14
Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 3.0 or Attribution-Noncommercial-No Derivative Works 3.0 will require attribution. You need to mention authors with the license, thats all. I am not the author:-) and do not represent any publishing house that is represented:-/ I think mentioning an actual author/name is desired.
user [154] · 2008-10-13
It has a nice CC license to the front. How do you attribute it? Interesting. CCd
http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/3.0/
user [2] · 2008-10-13
ccd, please go to
http://www.pariprashnena.com/discussion/755/licensing-of-books-on-iskconmediacom
user [2] · 2008-10-19
The name of the author is in the books themselves, but if you want them specifically to appear in each first page with the license you can get a pdf editor and change them. Once you finish you can upload them again to the server. Thank you for your help.
user [351] · 2008-12-18
[quote][cite] Pandu das:[/cite]I tried a to download one document last night and this morning, but was unable. I have a dialup connection that is pretty slow. I use a download manager thats worked well for me elsewhere. The document was about 2.5 megabytes. It started at a relatively fast speed, then slowed down a lot and finally stopped without finishing the download. I tried four or five times. Unfortunately the download cannot be resumed, requiring it to be started from the beginning if stopped. Its been a long time since Id previously seen an unresumable download.

Ill try using something else to download when I get the chance.[/quote]

Archive.org says FTP is more realiable way to download the files. But its hard to find how to do it. Here is how:

Download Ftp Client: http://filezilla-project.org/download.php (They have outdone themselves amazing update, dl the exe)

Install and Launch. After this do the following..

Goto File/Site Manager/New Site/ Type:
Iskconmedia
Host:
ia311207.us.archive.org
Port (LEAVE BLANK)
Goto Advanced Tab/Default Remote Directory put this:
/3/items/iskconmedia_acaryas/

Press Connect.

Transfer by double clicking the file, this is a new version of filezilla, even I am inpressed at the speed.
